applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused kernel for batch norm + bias + divide + swish
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented two-kernel Triton solution: (1) compute_batch_stats_kernel computes per-column batch mean/variance in training mode, (2) fused_bn_bias_div_swish_kernel fuses batch normalization, bias addition, division, and Swish activation. Reduced 5+ PyTorch elementwise kernels to 2 Triton kernels.

attempted_but_failed.name: Single fused kernel using running_mean/running_var
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incorrect - reference model uses training mode batch norm which computes batch statistics, not running statistics

notes: GEMM dominates at 94.6% - handled by hipBLASLt, not optimizable via Triton in this task
Elementwise ops reduced from 5+ PyTorch kernels to 2 Triton kernels
Speedup limited to ~1.02x because elementwise portion is only ~5-7% of total runtime
Key insight: Reference model uses training mode batch norm (computes batch statistics), not eval mode (running statistics)
BLOCK_SIZE=8192 for fused kernel (next_power_of_2 of out_features), BLOCK_SIZE=256 for batch stats kernel

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all produced no output—agent likely attempted overly complex changes (Triton GEMM, torch.compile graph fusion) that caused hangs or crashes. Need a conservative, incremental approac

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 crashed with no output — 4 consecutive failures (trials 2-5). The agent keeps attempting overly complex changes. Need an extremely conservative approach starting from the known-working trial 1
